items.headline: Wolves confirm Edwards has no structural damage in his knee, but he'll be out for at least a week
items.description:
DENVER (AP) — Minnesota All-Star Anthony Edwards will be sidelined for at least one week with a hyperextension and bone bruise in his left knee, a diagnosis the Timberwolves formally announced Monday before Game 5 of their first-round NBA playoff series at Denver.
Edwards had an MRI exam that confirmed the absence of structural damage, a relief to the Timberwolves after Edwards' injury occurred in the second quarter of their Game 4 victory over the Nuggets. Still, his availability for the second round if they advance will be in question. The team called his status week to week, so Edwards would likely miss at least the beginning of the next series before he's cleared to return.

items.headline: Carson Hocevar already living up to his reputation as NASCAR's newest star
items.description:
Carson Hocevar never stops thinking about how he can connect with NASCAR fans, whether through zany social media posts or cheering beside them in the grandstands.
He devised a unique way to greet NASCAR Nation after the first Cup Series win of his career.

items.headline: Arizona big man Motiejus Krivas returning to the Wildcats after Final Four run
items.description:
Arizona big man Motiejus Krivas is returning for the 2026-27 season, giving the Wildcats a key player from their Final Four run.
Arizona announced Krivas' return on Monday, less than a week after the departures of freshmen Brayden Burries and Koa Peat for the NBA draft.

items.headline: Modric's AC Milan season over after 'successful' cheekbone surgery: source
items.description: Luka Modric will not play again for AC Milan this season after undergoing surgery on a cheekbone fracture suffered in his team's goalless draw with Juventus, a source with knowledge...

items.headline: Braves rotation is reset as Weiss moves López to bullpen and says more changes could be coming
items.description:
ATLANTA (AP) — Right-hander Reynaldo López, who opened the season as Atlanta's No. 2 starter, is moving to the bullpen as Braves manager Walt Weiss shuffles his rotation for a series against Detroit.
More changes could be coming, with right-hander Spencer Strider possibly making his season debut in this weekend's series at Colorado after opening the season on the injured list because of a strained oblique.

items.headline: Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby enters treatment program for a gambling addiction, school says
items.description: LUBBOCK, Texas (AP) — Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby enters treatment program for a gambling addiction, school says.

items.headline: Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby enters treatment program for a gambling addiction following transfer
items.description:
LUBBOCK, Texas (AP) — Texas Tech quarterback Brendan Sorsby has taken an indefinite leave of absence from the team to enter a residential treatment program for a gambling addiction.
Texas Tech and Sorsby announced the move in a statement released on Monday.

items.description: Josh Hazlewood and Bhuvneshwar Kumar wreaked havoc as defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru skittled out Delhi Capitals for 75 before romping to a nine-wicket victory...

items.headline: Sabalenka avoids Madrid Open virus scare and Osaka upset. Gauff loses
items.description:
MADRID (AP) — Tennis players are facing an unknown opponent at the Madrid Open.
A stomach virus or food poisoning has affected Coco Gauff, Marin Cilic and a few others, causing some concern.
